title: Backslash traversal bypasses path-safety checks on Windows
reasoning: The code imports platform-specific `node:path`, but the traversal and symlink checks hard-code `/` when detecting parent paths and splitting path components. On Windows, `relative(root, outside)` uses backslashes, so a path like `..\outside.ts` would not match `back === '..'` or `back.startsWith('../')`; it can then be used for the hidden acceptance-test write. The tests assert the intended traversal rejection but only cover forward-slash traversal and symlink parents.
recommendation: Normalize and reject both `/` and `\` traversal separators for untrusted relative inputs, or use platform-aware checks with `path.sep` plus explicit rejection of alternate separators where appropriate. Add Windows-style traversal cases for `safeWorktreePath` and `assertSafeSourcePath`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current path-safety tests use `/tmp/outside.ts`, `../outside.ts`, and a forward-slash child path; they do not cover Windows-style `..\` traversal.
suggestedRegressionTest: Assert that `safeWorktreePath(worktree, '..\\outside.ts')` throws and that `changeCostGitCommandPlan` rejects a `srcDir` containing `..\\` traversal.
minimumFixScope: Harden path validation helpers and extend the path-safety tests with backslash traversal cases.

history.reasoning: The original source lines still exist but have been changed to reject backslashes: assertSafeSourcePath now checks srcDir.includes("\\") and splits on /[\\/]+/u, safeWorktreePath rejects rel.includes("\\"), checks back.startsWith("..\\"), and splits on /[\\/]+/u. The test evidence also moved from only forward-slash cases to include safeWorktreePath(worktree, "..\\outside.ts") and changeCostGitCommandPlan({srcDir:"..\\src"}). However the package export used by @codenuke/changecost currently resolves to packages/changecost/dist/changecost.js, and that current dist file still has the old slash-only checks. A direct import of the current exported module allowed both backslash inputs. The targeted vitest command could not run in this read-only sandbox because Vite attempted to write node_modules/.vite-temp, but the exported runtime evidence shows the issue remains open in the current executable package artifact until dist is rebuilt or consumers/tests are pointed at the fixed source.
commands: node --input-type=module -e 'import { safeWorktreePath, changeCostGitCommandPlan } from "./packages/changecost/dist/changecost.js"; for (const [name, fn] of [["safeWorktreePath", () => safeWorktreePath("/tmp/wt", "..\\\\outside.ts")], ["changeCostGitCommandPlan", () => changeCostGitCommandPlan({repo:"/repo", worktree:"/tmp/wt", ref:"HEAD", srcDir:"..\\\\src"})]]) { try { fn(); console.log(name + ": allowed"); } catch (e) { console.log(name + ": " + e.message); } }'

node --input-type=module -e 'const rel = String.raw`..\outside.ts`; import("./packages/changecost/dist/changecost.js").then(({safeWorktreePath, changeCostGitCommandPlan}) => { for (const [name, fn] of [["safeWorktreePath", () => safeWorktreePath("/tmp/wt", rel)], ["changeCostGitCommandPlan", () => changeCostGitCommandPlan({repo:"/repo", worktree:"/tmp/wt", ref:"HEAD", srcDir:String.raw`..\src`})]]) { try { const value = fn(); console.log(`${name}: allowed ${JSON.stringify(value)}`); } catch (e) { console.log(`${name}: ${e.message}`); } } })'
